title: He Hears The Cry Of The Sedge
content:
I wander by the edge
Of this desolate lake
Where wind cries in the sedge:
i(Until the axle break
That keeps the stars in their round,
And hands hurl in the deep
The banners of East and West,
And the girdle of light is unhound,
Your breast will not lie by the breast
Of your beloved in sleep.)
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "He Hears The Cry Of The Sedge"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
